symptoms.symptom_name: Rapidly enlarging firm nodule over six weeks, arising from a long-standing flat scaly patch (actinic keratosis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid growth of a firm nodule arising in a pre-existing actinic keratosis is the characteristic evolution of an invasive SCC.
BCC typically grows slowly over months to years; a six-week rapid transformation argues against it and toward SCC/keratoacanthoma.

symptoms.symptom_name: Thick adherent keratotic scale / cutaneous horn that detached, revealing a central ulcer

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyperkeratosis (a cutaneous horn) overlying a firm nodule that ulcerates is characteristic of a keratinising SCC; visible on close video though dermoscopy/biopsy is confirmatory.
BCC is characteristically pearly and translucent rather than thickly hyperkeratotic; a thick keratotic horn favours SCC.

symptoms.symptom_name: Central ulceration with a necrotic base, now heavily oozing dark venous blood after the crust detached

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ulceration and easy bleeding of a rapidly-growing indurated nodule reflect a tumour that has outgrown its blood supply — a bleeding, ulcerating lesion raises the malignancy concern that mandates biopsy.

symptoms.symptom_name: New tenderness/pain in the lump over the last 24 hours (previously painless while growing)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New tenderness in a previously painless nodule can reflect rapid growth, ulceration or perineural involvement — a high-risk feature favouring invasive SCC over an indolent lesion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Haemodynamically stable — blood pressure, heart rate, temperature, respiratory rate and oxygen saturation within acceptable limits (nurse-relayed)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: invasive cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma with acute haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal observations (allowing for anxiety-related mild hypertension and rate-controlled atrial fibrillation) indicate no significant blood loss or shock — the patient-obtainable data that justifies urgent-but-not-emergency triage rather than a 000 call.

symptoms.symptom_name: Daily apixaban 5 mg twice daily (direct oral anticoagulant) for atrial fibrillation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: invasive cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma with acute haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Anticoagulation prolongs bleeding from the fragile ulcerated tumour surface, explaining the persistent ooze and informing hemostasis, follow-up and the caution against reflexive cessation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lesion on chronically sun-exposed skin (dorsum of the hand) with a lifetime of sun exposure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cumulative UV exposure on the dorsal hand is a dominant risk factor for actinic keratosis and its progression to invasive SCC.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep induration with fixation/tethering of the nodule to underlying subcutaneous tissue (lacking free mobility)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deep induration and fixation to underlying tissue indicate deep invasion — a high-risk SCC feature; this is a clinician palpation finding, sealed as answer-key and not obtainable over telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpable firm 1.5 cm mobile left axillary lymph node

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A palpable regional node in the draining basin raises the possibility of nodal metastasis from a high-risk SCC and mandates urgent staging; requires clinician palpation and so is sealed as answer-key.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever and no surrounding spreading cellulitic erythema, warmth or purulence

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever, spreading erythema, warmth or purulence argues against an acute soft-tissue infection and against antibiotics; the surrounding-skin assessment is confirmed on clinician inspection but the patient/carer can report the absence of fever and spreading redness.

symptoms.symptom_name: Skin biopsy / excision histopathology (with regional nodal assessment)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Histology is the only way to confirm invasive SCC, grade it and definitively exclude keratoacanthoma, BCC and melanoma; unavailable in telehealth and the reason a fast-track excision/biopsy referral is mandatory.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Skin biopsy / excision with histopathology and margin assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive tissue diagnosis, grading and complete excision of the suspected high-risk SCC; the core reason a fast-track in-person skin-cancer/surgical referral is required. Cannot be performed via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person examination and dermoscopy by a skin/surgical clinician, including palpation of the primary and regional lymph nodes
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characterises the lesion, assesses depth/fixation and the palpable axillary node, and plans excision and staging; requires in-person assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Regional nodal assessment (axillary ultrasound +/- fine-needle aspiration) as directed by the specialist
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A palpable draining node in a high-risk SCC warrants staging of possible nodal metastasis, arranged through the fast-track surgical/oncology referral.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count (to check for significant blood loss) in the in-person setting
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Reasonable in person given heavy bleeding on anticoagulation to confirm no clinically significant anaemia; not an emergency test and not a telehealth action while the patient is stable.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Broad screening blood panel / coagulation studies ordered remotely
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated as a remote reflex for a controllable local bleed in a stable patient on a therapeutic DOAC; ordering broad bloods over telehealth here is unnecessary over-investigation.
